question.text: Equity Linked Savings Scheme (ELSS) has a statutory lock-in period of:

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** ELSS has a statutory lock-in of 3 years and provides tax benefit. Minimum 80% must be invested in equity and equity related instruments. ELSS provides deductions under Section 80C of the Income Tax Act, with maximum deduction of Rs. 1.5 lakh per financial year. However, this tax benefit is available only under the old income tax regime.
**The Trap:** 5 years is the lock-in for solution-oriented retirement/children's funds, not ELSS. The 3-year lock-in for ELSS is the shortest among all tax-saving instruments under Section 80C (PPF is 15 years, NSC is 5 years, etc.).
**Cross-Exam Context:** MF topics overlap with Series V-A, V-B. ELSS lock-in period is one of the most frequently tested facts in all MF exams.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** ELSS offers a dual benefit — tax saving and potential equity market returns. Highlight to clients that 3 years is the shortest lock-in among Section 80C options, but remind them the tax benefit under 80C applies only under the old tax regime.
